microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized, self-service system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution from manual to a scalable, developer-friendly model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability — delivered a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation motion across the platform.

Built an **internal AI agent** and restructured planning workflows to cut planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21%, compressing execution cycles and keeping the broader org at pace with engineering velocity.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating across engineering, ops, and partner teams end-to-end.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training that drove utilization across 14 key teams and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly by embedding LLM-powered tooling into developer workflows.

Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** by running 11+ structured user interviews with Azure service teams, translating customer workflow gaps into a prioritized, evidence-backed feature investment.

Improved data retrieval for AI-powered workflows by implementing metadata standards and migrating documentation to a **semantic search** platform, cutting lookup time by 83% and accelerating developer onboarding to new APIs.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams, removing the blockers that stalled go-to-market execution.

Defined a product strategy to eliminate operational toil during region launches — ran discovery with 20+ service teams, identified **81 hours of manual effort** per region, and built a roadmap to automate the highest-impact critical paths.

Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, giving leadership the instrumentation needed to target automation gaps and make data-driven prioritization decisions at platform scale.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping all dependencies across 1,200+ stations — de-risking the rollout before a single unit went live.

Prioritized a backlog of 40+ high-priority tickets across sprint cycles, facilitating Agile ceremonies that stabilized the **developer intake process** and unblocked engineering from weeks of accumulated ambiguity.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to ship automated **CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and enabling the team to iterate on robotics firmware at startup velocity.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end proposal workflow — built a new CRM process that managed scoping and invoicing for 200+ monthly proposals and removed the friction killing conversion at every stage.

Cut **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy: optimized the website and Google Business profile to match buyer intent and improve top-of-funnel performance.

Identified and closed margin leaks by conducting a profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records**, giving leadership the pricing model precision needed to improve unit economics across all service verticals.

tailoring_notes: Wove in 'self-service' and 'developer-friendly model' (JD: 'no-code workflows, APIs, developers') into microsoft_ft bullet 2 — directly mirrors Reducto's platform surface language.
Used 'intent-based YAML generation,' 'semantic search,' and 'LLM-powered tooling' (JD: 'document intelligence,' 'AI-native development,' 'proprietary models') across microsoft_2023 bullets 2 and 3.
Embedded 'go-to-market,' 'pricing model,' and 'unit economics' (JD: 'pricing, packaging, PLG motions, enterprise expansion') into microsoft_2022 bullet 1 and pro_painters bullets 2–3.
Used 'de-risking the rollout before a single unit went live' (JD: 'de-risking decisions early') in amazon_robotics bullet 1 to mirror founding PM accountability language.

Framed microsoft_ft bullet 4 around 'compressing execution cycles' and 'keeping the broader org at pace with engineering velocity' — directly echoing JD's 'AI-native development' and 'extraordinary velocity end to end' mandate.
